subject: Geography
framework: 2023 Indiana Academic Standards – Middle School Geography

standards.text: Identify and describe the major physical features of Earth, including mountains, plains, plateaus, rivers, and oceans.
Explain the theory of plate tectonics and how it shapes Earth's surface through earthquakes, volcanoes, and mountain building.
Describe the water cycle and explain how water moves through Earth's systems.
Identify the major climate zones of Earth and explain the factors that influence climate, including latitude, elevation, and ocean currents.
Describe the major biomes of the world and the relationship between climate and vegetation.
Explain the processes of weathering and erosion and how they shape landforms over time.
Describe different types of natural disasters and explain their geographic causes and effects.
Explain how physical geography affects human settlement patterns and economic activities.
Describe patterns of population distribution and explain factors that influence where people live.
Explain the causes and effects of human migration, including push and pull factors.
Describe how cultural traits such as language, religion, and customs vary across regions and are spread through cultural diffusion.
Explain the process of urbanization and compare urban and rural areas in terms of population, economy, and land use.
Describe how humans adapt to, modify, and depend on the physical environment.
Analyze population data using demographic indicators such as birth rate, death rate, and population density.
Use latitude and longitude to identify absolute locations on maps and globes.
Interpret different types of maps, including political, physical, thematic, and topographic maps.
Use map elements such as scale, compass rose, legend, and title to gather information.
Explain how different map projections represent Earth's surface and why distortions occur.
Describe the geographic tools and technologies used to collect, analyze, and display geographic data, including GIS and GPS.
Identify the hemispheres, continents, and major oceans using maps and globes.
Locate and describe the physical and human characteristics of the major world regions.
Compare and contrast the cultures, governments, and economies of different world regions.
Identify the major countries, capitals, and physical features of each continent.
Explain how trade and transportation networks connect world regions economically.
Describe how political boundaries are established and how they change over time.
Explain the role of major rivers, mountain ranges, and bodies of water in shaping civilizations and trade.
Classify natural resources as renewable or nonrenewable and explain their distribution across regions.
Describe the causes and effects of climate change and its impact on global geography.
Explain the importance of conservation and sustainability in managing natural resources.
Describe the major ecosystems of the world and explain how living things depend on their environment.
Analyze how human activities such as deforestation, pollution, and urbanization affect the environment.
